channel.title: Proxy on Margrop Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Proxy on Margrop Blog

item.title: Use LXC containers to build V2Ray and Caddy services on Alpine Linux to provide IPv6-based SSL WebSocket proxy
item.description: Preface In the modern Internet environment, proxy services are becoming more and more important, especially when it is necessary to break through geographical restrictions and improve privacy protection. This article will introduce in detail how to use V2Ray and Caddy to build an SSL WebSocket proxy service provided through IPv6 and domain name on the LXC container of the Alpine Linux 3.19 operating system. Environment preparation Make sure your LXC container is running and has an IPv6 address assigned.

item.title: Bypassing the Firewall or Setting Up a Server Farm: V2Ray Configuration with WebSocket + TLS + Web + CDN
item.description: v2ray The most secure configuration currently is WebSocket + TLS + Web + CDN. It uses port 443: direct access shows a website, but after connecting with a client it becomes a proxy tool. From the outside it looks like an electric shaver, but it is actually a hair dryer.

item.title: Proxy Only for GitHub git clone
item.description:
git config --global http.https://github.com.proxy socks5://127.0.0.1:1080
git config --global https.https://github.com.proxy socks5://127.0.0.1:1080
